title: General Knowledge Sample Quiz
description: A short sample quiz covering general science, geography, and history — load this to confirm Quiz Engine is working before trying your own file.

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.explanation: Au comes from the Latin word for gold, 'aurum.' Ag is silver, and Go/Gd are not real element symbols.

questions.question: Which planet in our solar system has the most moons?
questions.explanation: Saturn has the most confirmed moons of any planet in the solar system, having overtaken Jupiter as new moons were discovered.

questions.question: A recipe calls for doubling the yield. If the original recipe uses 3/4 cup of flour, how much flour is needed for the doubled batch?
questions.explanation: Doubling 3/4 cup means multiplying by 2: 3/4 × 2 = 6/4 = 1 1/2 cups.

questions.question: Which ocean is the largest by surface area?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest ocean, covering more area than all the continents combined.

questions.question: In which century did the printing press become widespread in Europe, following Gutenberg's innovations?
questions.explanation: Gutenberg introduced his movable-type printing press around 1440, and the technology spread rapidly across Europe through the rest of the 15th century.

questions.question: A train travels 240 miles in 4 hours at a constant speed. What is its average speed?
questions.explanation: Average speed is distance divided by time: 240 miles ÷ 4 hours = 60 mph.

questions.question: Which gas makes up the largest percentage of Earth's atmosphere by volume?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the atmosphere, far more than oxygen (about 21%) or the trace gases.

questions.question: A museum exhibit is most likely to use a timeline display when its primary goal is to:
options: Show how an event or technology developed over time
List the heaviest objects in the collection
questions.explanation: Timelines are designed specifically to show sequence and development over time, which is why they're the natural choice for showing historical progression.
